title: It's election day — guide me through it
subtitle: Step-by-step walkthrough of voting day

intro.title: Let's make sure today goes smoothly.
intro.body: We'll cover what to carry, what to expect at the booth, how the EVM works, and what to do if something goes wrong. About 10 minutes.

checkName.title: Quick check — is your name on the roll?
checkName.body: Even if you've voted before, do this 5-second check now. Better to know in advance than at the booth.

checkBooth.title: You can only vote at one specific booth.
checkBooth.body:
Your polling station is shown on the same Electoral Search page. Note the **booth name and address** — Google Maps it now.

If you're at the wrong booth, you cannot vote. They will not redirect you.

documents.title: Tick what you have.
documents.body: You need only ONE of these — EPIC is preferred but any work:

leaveBehind.title: Don't bring these into the booth.
leaveBehind.body:
**Mobile phones are not allowed inside the polling booth.** You'll need to switch off and submit it at the entrance, or leave it home.

Also leave behind: campaign material, anything with party symbols, and any food/drink.

atBooth.title: Here's exactly what happens.
atBooth.body:
**1.** Stand in queue. Senior citizens and PwD voters get priority — wave them ahead.

**2.** First polling officer checks your name on the roll and your photo ID.

**3.** Second officer marks indelible ink on your **left index finger**.

**4.** Third officer hands you a slip and points you to the EVM compartment.

**5.** You vote. We'll cover that next.

evmStep.title: The EVM is just two buttons.
evmStep.body:
**1.** Find your candidate's name and symbol on the Ballot Unit.

**2.** Press the blue button next to them. **One press, one vote.**

**3.** A red light glows next to your choice. A beep confirms.

**4.** Look at the VVPAT machine on the side — a paper slip prints showing your candidate's name and symbol. It's visible for 7 seconds.

**5.** That slip drops automatically into a sealed box. You don't take it.

You cannot vote again. The machine locks for you.

vvpatTip.title: Verify your VVPAT slip carefully.
vvpatTip.body:
The 7-second VVPAT window is your only verification that the machine recorded your vote correctly.

If the printed slip doesn't match the candidate you chose, **call the Presiding Officer immediately**. They can take action including challenging the EVM.

choices.label: My name's not on the roll
choices.sublabel: I checked and was told it's not there

nameMissing.title: If your name's missing on election day:
nameMissing.body:
**1.** Ask the polling officer to search alternate spellings of your name.

**2.** Ask to see the marked copy of the electoral roll yourself.

**3.** Request to file a **Tendered Vote** — you can vote, but it's kept separate.

**4.** File a written complaint with the Sector Officer or Returning Officer that day.

**5.** Call **1950** while at the booth.

complete.title: You're ready. Go vote.
complete.summary:
Your finger gets inked. Your VVPAT slip drops. Democracy advances by exactly one vote — yours.

Thank you for showing up today.
